channel.title: Jiǎoluò Shūwū · 角落書屋
channel.description: Notes on Chinese language and civilisation — characters, vocabulary, grammar, history, philosophy, and the world they shaped.

item.title: 风 · wind; style; custom
item.description: The character that moves crops, carries seeds, and became the Chinese word for cultural character itself.

item.title: 来 · to come, to arrive, toward the speaker
item.description: Oracle bone wheat plant turned direction-word — 来 pulls everything toward the speaker, including time itself.

item.title: 其 · its, that, their
item.description: A winnowing basket that became the pronoun holding classical Chinese together — the silent 'its' behind thousands of literary phrases.

item.title: 去 · to go, to leave, to depart, to remove
item.description: The character that marks every departure — from leaving the house to the euphemism for death, 去 is the verb of things moving away.

item.title: 上 · above, up, to go up, to mount
item.description: The most productive spatial primitive in Chinese — 上 starts as a notch above a line and ends up meaning everything from logging on to getting addicted.

item.title: 下 · below, down, to descend, to issue
item.description: The mirror image of 上 — a notch below the line that governs everything from going downstairs to issuing imperial decrees.

item.title: 信 · trust, faith, credibility
item.description: A person standing by their word — one of the most visually transparent characters in the language, and the virtue that holds every contract, conversation, and relationship together.

item.title: 义 · righteousness, duty, justice
item.description: The Confucian virtue of doing what is morally right — and the simplification that erased a sheep, a self, and the entire picture of what righteous form looks like.

item.title: 雨 · rain
item.description: The oracle bone shows rain falling from the sky — one of the most transparent pictographs in the script, and the radical that organizes all Chinese weather.

item.title: 云 · cloud
item.description: The simplified 云 is older than the traditional 雲 — a rare case where the modern form recovers the ancient one.

item.title: 智 · wisdom, moral intelligence
item.description: The Confucian virtue of knowing what is right to do — and, separately, the Buddhist prajñā that sees through the nature of things altogether.

item.title: 家庭 · Family Structure
item.description: Chinese kinship terminology is a precision instrument — each relative has one specific term, and that precision maps an obligation network, not just a family tree.

item.title: 面子实践 · Face in Practice
item.description: Face in Chinese social life is not an attitude — it is a recurring set of transactions with specific mechanics: how face is given, taken, lost, and preserved in concrete situations.

item.title: 着 · the continuous aspect marker
item.description: Three distinct jobs: holding a state, linking two actions, and commanding attention — all from a single particle that beginners collapse into one.

item.title: 道德与伦理 · Morality and Ethics — A Reading Path
item.description: Chinese ethics is not a checklist of personal virtues. It is a description of how one should relate to specific others — and what happens when those relationships are tested.

item.title: 地理与语言 · Geography and Language — A Reading Path
item.description: The rivers shaped the civilization; geography fractured the language; one writing system held it together.

item.title: 文学路径 · Literary China — A Reading Path
item.description: Chinese literature is not a sequence of styles that replaced each other. It is an accumulation. This path follows the layering forward in time.

item.title: 宗教地图 · The Religious Map — A Reading Path
item.description: Chinese religiosity doesn't fit Western categories. Most people practice elements of Confucianism, Daoism, Buddhism, folk religion, and ancestor veneration simultaneously — a map of how these traditions relate.

item.title: 自然 · nature; natural; of course
item.description: Laozi placed 自然 at the center of his cosmology: the Way models itself on what is so of itself — no outside force required.

item.title: 产 · to produce, give birth, property, output
item.description: From birth to bounty: a character that spans biological generation and industrial output, linking the miracle of birth to the logic of production and wealth.

item.title: 告 · to tell, to announce, to report
item.description: A mouth aimed upward: the character for delivering words to authority — and the root of advertising, accusations, and warnings.

item.title: 广 · wide, broad, to spread
item.description: A radical and a word: the cliff-shelter that became the prefix for everything expansive — from Guangzhou to Guangdong to radio broadcasts.

item.title: 品 · article, grade, character, to taste
item.description: Three mouths stacked: a character about multiplicity, evaluation, and the discerning assessment of quality — from product grades to moral character.

item.title: 图 · map, diagram, to plan, to seek
item.description: A character for diagrams, maps, intentions, and plots — anything that represents the world at one remove, or seeks to reshape it.

item.title: 之 · of, going, this — the classical connective
item.description: The most-written character in classical Chinese — a possessive particle, pronoun, and verb fossilized into pure grammar.

item.title: 笔画 · strokes — the building blocks of Chinese characters
item.description: Every Chinese character is built from a small set of standard strokes written in a prescribed order. Stroke count determines dictionary order; stroke order determines how a character looks when written by hand.

item.title: 部首 · radicals — the indexing keys of Chinese characters
item.description: Every Chinese character belongs to a radical family. Radicals are the organizing principle of dictionaries, the structural DNA of characters, and the fastest shortcut to guessing what an unfamiliar word means.

item.title: 产品 · product, manufactured good
item.description: The standard Chinese word for product — what production yields and commerce moves. From factory floor to digital marketplace, 产品 names the output of organized human effort.

item.title: 拼音 · the romanization system for Mandarin
item.description: Pinyin is the official romanization of Mandarin — the system that maps Chinese sounds to the Latin alphabet. It is a pronunciation guide, not a writing system, and it does not replace characters.

item.title: 生产 · to produce, production, manufacturing
item.description: The foundational act of economic life — and, in China's Marxist political vocabulary, the act from which all social relations flow. Life gives rise to production; production sustains life.
